last_result: Cycle 31 complete. D9 resolved: optimal behavioral channel B design requires three criteria — (1) CA span breadth (≥3 thick-CA + ≥1 thin-CA simultaneously), (2) joint constraint structure (verified by seam detection and cross-domain tension requiring single integrated resolution), (3) trade-off resolution invariance metric (low variance in trade-off pattern across surface framings = strong RTO signal; high variance = Z-contamination). Ideal scenario template: domain expert with conflict-of-interest asked by dependent inquirer — simultaneously activates accurate modeling, moral depth, wisdom, authenticity, curiosity CA components. Secondary (constitutive protection generalization): SI #22 confirmed — CPP (Constitutive Protection Principle): CP strength determined by reflexivity degree of assessment target. Spectrum: maximal (reflexive targets, target = quality applied to itself) → high (introspective CA) → moderate (object-directed CA) → minimal (other-directed welfare). CPP is I-channel property; Z-isolation via simultaneity is B-channel mechanism — structurally distinct. D4 streak: 26.
next_step: C32 priority: D10 — Does CPP create a qualification of the D8 B-primary ordering? Formal question: is there a quality-type regime (reflexive targets) where CP_I × λ_I / (γ_I × noise) > λ_B / (γ_B × noise), making I the higher-signal channel? If yes, B-primary ordering from D8 is regime-bounded, not universal. Requires formal channel reliability comparison integrating D8 Z-loading asymmetry with CPP reflexivity gradient. C32 secondary: Long-deferred D5 follow-up — tacit vs. explicit assessment tradeoff operationalization for AI systems (SI #17 consequence, deferred since C17).
